headlines.title: World Cup in the Bay Area: Jordan vs. Algeria in Santa Clara
headlines.description: World Cup action returns to the Bay Area Monday night when Jordan takes on Algeria in group stage play. Kickoff for the Group J match at San Francisco Bay Area Stadium is scheduled for 8 p.m. Both nations came up short in their World Cup openers last Tuesday. Jordan fell to Austria, 3-1, while Algeria was blanked by Argentina, 3-0. Monday night’s clash will mark the fourth World Cup match at the South Bay stadium. One more group stage match – Paraguay vs. Australia – is scheduled for Thursday, followed by a Round of 32 contest featuring the United States on July 1. San Jose 14 hours ago 2026 World Cup Jun 17 San Jose Jun 20 Organizers to add 4th screen in San Pedro Square for World Cup watch parties

headlines.title: San Jose considers extending parking meter hours, doubling rates downtown
headlines.description: A night out in downtown San Jose could soon cost drivers more. The city is considering a proposal that would extend parking meter hours and increase parking rates as officials look to capitalize on increased nightlife activity downtown. Under the proposal, metered parking would be enforced until 9 p.m. instead of 6 p.m. The hourly rate would also double from $2 to $4. City officials say about 1,600 parking meters would be affected. Some meters, including those in Japantown and east of City Hall, would be exempt from the changes. The proposed $4 hourly rate would match the cost of parking in several city-owned garages. San Jose’s smart meters allow drivers to pay by tapping a card or paying through a mobile app, eliminating the need to carry coins or cash. The city estimates extending meter hours and increasing rates could generate about $1.2 million in additional annual revenue.

headlines.title: Man dies after San Jose e-scooter crash, police say
headlines.description: A man injured in an electric scooter crash in San Jose has died, marking the city’s 22nd traffic-related fatality of 2026, police said. The crash occurred at about 4:56 p.m. June 9 near Commercial Street and Oakland Road, San Jose police noted. According to a preliminary investigation by the San Jose Police Department, the rider was traveling eastbound in a westbound bike lane on a black Segway electric scooter when he lost control at a high rate of speed and fell. Police said the rider was not wearing a helmet. He was taken to a local hospital with life-threatening injuries and later stabilized. Investigators learned Tuesday that the man died from his injuries on June 13, police said. The Santa Clara County Medical Examiner’s Office will release the victim’s identity. Anyone with information about the crash is asked to contact the Detective Banzon of the San Jose Police Department’s Traffic Investigations Unit at: 4661@sanjoseca.gov or (408) 277-4654.

headlines.title: Stolen memorial monument from Vietnamese Heritage Garden in San Jose recovered
headlines.description: Authorities recovered a stolen memorial monument that was stolen from the Vietnamese Heritage Garden in San Jose Sunday afternoon. Police said some suspects stole the memorial monument from the garden located in Kelley Park on Roberts Avenue. With the help of a neighborhood patrol unit, officers later found the statue leaning against a rocky area. Investigators were trying to figure out how the thieves stole the statue and why.

headlines.title: Fans pack San Pedro Square for Spain-Saudi Arabia World Cup watch party
headlines.description: San Jose’s San Pedro Square continues to be a hotspot for World Cup watch parties. Sunday, soccer fans packed the plaza to watch the Spain-Saudi Arabia match. Fans were proudly wearing their team colors from head to toe. The game was exciting, with a lot of action in the first half. Marianne Favro has the full report in the video above.

headlines.title: Lasers will light up the sky over San Francisco for Pride, World Cup, July 4
headlines.description: Starting Sunday night, multicolor lasers will beam light into the sky over downtown San Francisco as part of a new public art project called 7×7. This display will run overnight from June 21 through July 4 and is intended to celebrate San Francisco’s Pride festival, the FIFA World Cup, and America’s 250th birthday celebration on the 4th of July. The display is slated to run every night from sunset to sunrise. These lasers were organized by the nonprofit Illuminate, which also leads the other major light displays in the city such as the Bay Bridge lights and the Market Street Pride Flag lights. San Francisco Mayor Daniel Lurie’s Office also collaborated on bringing this project forward. This display will be projected from 49 cannons (representing the 49 square miles of San Francisco), pointing towards the sky from the city’s Civic Center plaza. Illuminate founder Ben Davis explained that the lasers will be rainbow colors to celebrate Pride in San Francisco, and on the Fourth of July, they will be red, white, and blue. But in between those times, he said organizers may experiment with other colors and patterns. Based on how other laser displays have gone in San Francisco, Davis expects people will be able to see this light display from miles away, even as far as mountaintops across the bay. He said that how visible the lasers are may come down to how the fog behaves, and organizers will learn more through the different weather patterns that unfold over the next 14 days.

headlines.title: NWS warns of hazardous conditions along Northern California coastline
headlines.description: The entire Northern California coastline is under beach hazard warnings. The National Weather Service warns that, for the next few days, the danger will be higher than normal. San Francisco’s Baker Beach is notorious for having exceptionally strong sneaker waves. The danger of those waves will be higher than normal, according to the NWS. Santa Cruz State Parks lifeguards said teams have been busy with water rescues for more than a week. Two Bay Area college students in their 20s were napping on a beach near Santa Cruz on June 10 when they were swept out to sea by a sneaker wave and drowned. On Thursday, a mother and a child were pulled from the water, after they were hit by a sneaker wave at Baker Beach. At last report, they were hospitalized in critical condition with non-life-threatening injuries. San Francisco Jun 18 Santa Cruz County Jun 16 Experienced Bay Area crabber Volark Keo is a moderator for the Pacifica Pier Fishing and Crabbing group. He is charge of warning crabbers about the surf dangers in social media advisories. “Basically, respect the wave. For me, I pray before during and after, and always keep my head on a swivel, no matter what. And if you have a bad feeling, get out. Crab another day. Live another day,” he said. The National Weather Service advises beach goers to stay far from the water’s edge, and off jetties, rocks and piers, and as always never turn their backs on the ocean. The NWS says the Surf Hazard Advisories will remain in effect until at least Wednesday evening.

headlines.title: Berkeley celebrates Juneteenth with festival honoring history
headlines.description: The 39th annual Berkeley Juneteenth festival brought people out to honor history and celebrate culture. The festival commemorates the emancipation of enslaved African Americans. “This is the one day everyone comes back to south Berkeley, those who are still here, those who have left come, we see everyone we haven’t seen in a long time and it’s an opportunity to express the rich cultural diversity that exists in south Berkeley and the strong heritage, multi racial heritage,” said Duane DeJoie, with the Berkeley Juneteenth Celebration Festival Board. That includes Jerome Patterson, who spent years in Berkeley and has since moved away, but visited the city just for this event. “For this community the people the culture everyone. This is Berkeley, this is what it’s all about. Just a mix of people blending in and having a good time,” Patterson said. San Francisco Jun 20 Juneteenth Jun 19 Juneteenth Jun 19 The Adeline Business Corridor was an important part of starting the festival 39 years ago, and Sunday, people came out to enjoy vendors, stages, food, music, literature and more. “I hope to take a little more happiness and lot of respect for my culture and thankfulness and to be grateful that I’m here and able to celebrate,” said Ginger, from Hercules. Organizers say they expect thousands of people to be a part of the day. “The Juneteenth festival has been one of the things that we most love coming out and being part of the community and getting to be part of the history of Juneteenth and they have got some great food and awesome stands out and great to support black owned businesses,” Anna Martin said.

headlines.title: Driver injured after SUV crashes into home in San Jose
headlines.description: A driver was taken to the hospital after an SUV crashed into a home in San Jose Sunday afternoon, according to the fire department. San Jose Fire said they received a call at 3:30 p.m. regarding a SUV that crashed into a home at 90 North Buena Vista Avenue. Authorities said the male driver, who was the sole occupant of the vehicle, had to be extricated from it. He was taken to the hospital with non-life-threatening injuries. Officials added nobody was home at the time of the crash and no other injuries were reported.

headlines.title: Police investigate shooting that left 2 injured in San Jose
headlines.description: San Jose police said they are investigating a shooting that left two people hurt Saturday night. Police said they received a call at around 10:38 p.m. in the area of Great Oaks Parkway and Lexington Avenue regarding a person who had been shot. The caller told communications their front passenger, a man, was suffering from at least one gunshot wound. Officials said the victim was taken to the hospital with non-life-threatening injuries, and the shooting was said to have occurred somewhere else in the city. At 10:58 p.m., police said they received another call regarding an additional victim. According to authorities, this victim was in the area of Roeder Road and Azucar Road and arrived at a local hospital with at least one gunshot wound. Police added his injuries were non-life-threatening. The police department said this is an ongoing investigation.

headlines.title: CHP investigates fatal crash on Hwy. 17 in Santa Cruz County
headlines.description: An investigation is underway after a fatal crash that prompted the closure of the right lane on southbound Highway 17 in Santa Cruz County on Saturday afternoon, the California Highway Patrol said. CHP said a vehicle crashed into a tree on southbound Highway 17, near Vine Hill Road, just after noon. Authorities reported the crash ended in fatality, and advised motorists the right lane of the highway would be closed for an unknown period of time while officers investigate. CHP has not released more information on this incident.

headlines.title: Organizers to add 4th screen in San Pedro Square for World Cup watch parties
headlines.description: Watch party organizers at San Pedro Square are preparing for the upcoming USMNT match in Santa Clara on July 1. Every World Cup match, the crowds in San Pedro Square seem to get bigger and bigger. When Mexico played against South Korea Thursday night, more than 25,000 World Cup watchers packed into San Pedro Square, filling bars, restaurants, and every inch of the fan zone, where three outdoor screens are set up. When USMNT beat Australia Friday, it was another record turnout. Bettina Baros, the co-owner of the Old Wagon Saloon, opened an outdoor VIP area to manage foot traffic during the matches, offering prime seating in front of the big screens. She’s already getting calls for VIP reservations when Team USA takes the pitch in Santa Clara on July 1. “July 1, oh yeah, that’s already booked. It’s going to be packed,” she said. San Pedro Square currently has three screens set up to broadcast live matches; one on each end of San Pedro Street, and one along Sharks Way. Anticipating even bigger crowds, organizers are planning to add a fourth screen along St. James Street. “That is the goal, to have it approved and ready for next week, when Mexico plays on Wednesday,” San Jose Sports Authority Executive Director John Poch said. The Bay Area Host Committee projects tourism, jobs, and spending from World Cup fans will bring economic gains to Santa Clara County estimated between $270 million and $360 million. The city of San Jose is likely to approve the permit to add a fourth screen to San Pedro Square on Monday.
